name: Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito
name_original: Saelices desde Montalbo — circular por la Laguna de El Hito
region_intro: Best cycling routes Montalbo offer a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. The region features routes that traverse areas around natural water bodies, such as the Laguna de El Hito. Terrain includes rolling hills with moderate elevation gains, providing varied cycling experiences. The area's routes connect local villages and natural features, suitable for exploring by touring bicycle. Best touring cycling routes around Montalbo The most popular touring cycling route is Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete, circling a significant lagoon. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Saelices – Fountain of the Five Spouts loop from Saelices, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.3 km) path featuring local landmarks and varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguna de El Hito – Villarejo de Fuentes loop from Barranco de los Moros, a 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail leading through areas around the Laguna de El Hito, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Montalbo is defined by routes connecting villages, featuring natural lagoons, and offering rolling terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 7 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Montalbo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Montalbo?
A: Montalbo offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, characterized by rolling hills with moderate elevation gains. Routes often traverse areas around natural water bodies, such as the Laguna de El Hito, and connect local villages, providing varied cycling experiences.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Montalbo?
A: Yes, Montalbo features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084296488/saelices-from-montalbo-circular-by-the-laguna-de-el-hito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 40 km long and circles a significant lagoon. Another well-liked choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices – Fountain of the Five Spouts loop from Saelices&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.3 km path.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging touring cycling routes in Montalbo?
A: For those seeking a more demanding ride, Montalbo offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39554918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices – Carrascosa del Campo loop from Montalbo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 63 km and features significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarejo de Fuentes – Laguna de El Hito loop from Villarejo de Fuentes&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 56 km.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Montalbo?
A: There are 5 dedicated touring cycling routes in Montalbo, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes have been explored by over 7 touring cyclists using komoot.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect for touring cycling routes in Montalbo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Montalbo cater to different ability levels. You'll find 3 moderate routes, perfect for a steady ride, and 2 difficult routes for those looking for a greater challenge with more significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: Are there any natural features or landmarks to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in Montalbo feature natural water bodies, most notably the Laguna de El Hito. You can explore areas around this significant lagoon on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Hito – Villarejo de Fuentes loop from Barranco de los Moros&lt;/a&gt;. The region's landscape is defined by rolling terrain and connections between local villages.

Q: What nearby attractions can I visit in the Montalbo area?
A: Beyond cycling, the Montalbo area offers several interesting attractions. You can explore historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5399123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segóbriga Archaeological Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segóbriga&lt;/a&gt;, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6966258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amphitheatre&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider visiting the gorges of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5050337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Cigüela&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Río Cigüela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about Montalbo?
A: Touring cyclists who have explored Montalbo often highlight the diverse landscape and the opportunity to cycle around natural lagoons. The routes are appreciated for connecting local villages and offering varied terrain suitable for touring bicycles.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Laguna de El Hito?
A: Yes, the Laguna de El Hito is a prominent feature in Montalbo's cycling landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084296488/saelices-from-montalbo-circular-by-the-laguna-de-el-hito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito&lt;/a&gt; route directly circles this significant lagoon. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Hito – Villarejo de Fuentes loop from Barranco de los Moros&lt;/a&gt;, which also leads through areas around the lagoon.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in Montalbo?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Montalbo varies depending on the distance and difficulty. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084296488/saelices-from-montalbo-circular-by-the-laguna-de-el-hito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 14 minutes, while the longer, more difficult routes can take significantly longer, up to 4 hours and 43 minutes for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarejo de Fuentes – Laguna de El Hito loop from Villarejo de Fuentes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that start directly from Montalbo village?
A: Yes, several routes offer convenient starting points from Montalbo. The popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084296488/saelices-from-montalbo-circular-by-the-laguna-de-el-hito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito&lt;/a&gt; begins in Montalbo, as does the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39554918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices – Carrascosa del Campo loop from Montalbo&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Viewpoint of the Natural Reserve of the Lagoon of El Hito.
Ruins of the castle of Saelices. Probably the construction that can be seen today dates from the 16th century.

The Segóbriga Archaeological Park is a fascinating historical site located in Saelices, in the province of Cuenca, Spain. This park allows visitors to explore the ruins of the ancient Roman city of Segóbriga, which was an important urban center in the region during the 2nd century BC and beyond.
Among the most notable archaeological remains are an amphitheater, a theater, thermal baths, aqueducts, homes and other public and private buildings that offer a detailed insight into daily life in Roman times. In addition, the park has an Interpretation Center that provides additional information and historical contexts to enrich the visit. 🌄🚶‍♀️😎🚴🚵
